HexGuide.title: Hexagonal Guide
HexGuide.generalDesc: On a hexagonal grid, pieces move along 3 main axes (6 directions) instead of 2.
HexGuide.pawnDesc: Moves 1 hex forward. Captures on the 2 forward diagonal hexes (same color).
HexGuide.rookDesc: Moves any distance in 6 orthogonal directions (through hex sides).
HexGuide.bishopDesc: Moves any distance in 6 diagonal directions (through hex corners).
HexGuide.knightDesc: L-shape: 2 steps orthogonal, then 1 step at 60° (jumps over others).
HexGuide.kingDesc: 1 step in any of the 12 directions (6 orthogonal + 6 diagonal).
HexGuide.queenDesc: Combines rook and bishop movement (12 directions total).
HexGuide.note: Note: This editor follows axial coordinate logic. Actual movement depends on your own logic or the chosen variant (e.g. Glinski vs McCooey).

Play.kingWarning: Place both kings on the board to enable move validation
Play.title: Play Yourself

Piece.title: Design your <accent>Chess Pieces</accent>
Piece.description: Create pixel-perfect chess pieces with our intuitive 64x64 editor.
Piece.confirmInvert: Inverting the colors will overwrite the current design. Continue?
Piece.pieceEditorBetaMessage: The piece editor is currently in beta and only available to selected creators. We are working hard to enable pixel-perfect customization for everyone!
Piece.visualLogicDescription: Define how your piece moves by connecting blocks.

diffX.label: Horizontal Step
diffX.desc: The number of squares the piece moves horizontally. Positive is right, negative is left.

diffY.label: Vertical Step
diffY.desc: The number of squares the piece moves vertically. Positive is forward, negative is backward.

absDiffX.label: Horizontal Distance
absDiffX.desc: The absolute horizontal distance, regardless of left or right.

absDiffY.label: Vertical Distance
absDiffY.desc: The absolute vertical distance, regardless of forward or backward.

dist.label: Distance
dist.desc: The Chebyshev distance (square grids) or hex distance from the starting square to the target square.

cooldown.label: Cooldown
cooldown.desc: The piece's current cooldown counter. When above 0, the piece cannot move.

charge.label: Charge
charge.desc: A general-purpose numeric variable for the piece. Can be used to track charges, energy, or similar state.

mode.label: Mode
mode.desc: A general-purpose numeric variable representing the piece's current mode or state.

Logic.title: Logic Editor
Logic.variableTooltip: This field only accepts variables. Drag one from the 'Variables' category.

descriptions.onIsCaptured: Triggered when a piece is captured.
descriptions.onCaptured: Triggered when this piece captures another piece.
descriptions.onThreat: Triggered when the piece is threatened.
descriptions.onEnvironment: Triggered based on the board environment.
descriptions.onVar: Triggered when a variable meets a condition.
descriptions.cooldown: Applies a cooldown to the piece.
descriptions.transformation: Transforms the piece into another type.
descriptions.kill: Immediately removes the piece from the game.
descriptions.win: Ends the game immediately with a win for the owner of this piece.
descriptions.explode: Removes pieces within a radius around this piece.
descriptions.tellUser: Displays a speech bubble from the piece with a custom message.

LegalNotice.title: Legal Notice
LegalNotice.legalBasis: Information in accordance with § 5 DDG (German Digital Services Act):
LegalNotice.disputeText: We are neither obliged nor willing to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

PrivacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
PrivacyPolicy.controllerText:
Responsible for data processing on this website:

Lasse Thoroe — Chessperiment
Ahlerfeld 29
38527 Meine
Germany

Email: contact.chessperiment@gmail.com
Website: https://chessperiment.app

No data protection officer has been appointed as this is not legally required.
PrivacyPolicy.generalText:
We take the protection of your personal data very seriously. We process personal data only to the extent necessary to provide a functional website and our services. All processing is carried out in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the German Federal Data Protection Act (BDSG), and the Telecommunications and Digital Services Data Protection Act (TDDDG).

This privacy policy explains what data we collect, how we use it, and what rights you have regarding your data.
PrivacyPolicy.sslText: For security reasons and to protect the transmission of confidential content, this website uses SSL/TLS encryption. You can recognize an encrypted connection by the "https://" prefix and the lock icon in your browser's address bar. When SSL/TLS encryption is active, data you transmit to us cannot be read by third parties.
PrivacyPolicy.klaroText: We use Klaro!, an open-source consent management platform, to obtain and manage your consent for cookies and similar storage technologies. On your first visit, a banner appears allowing you to accept or reject individual categories of data processing. Your consent preferences are stored locally in your browser. You can change your preferences at any time by clicking the cookie settings link in the website footer. This consent management is required under § 25 TDDDG. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR (legal obligation).
PrivacyPolicy.sessionText: When you log in, a technically necessary session cookie (JSON Web Token) is set to maintain your authenticated session. This cookie is essential for the website's core functionality and does not require consent under § 25(2) TDDDG. It is deleted when you log out or close your browser. No tracking or advertising cookies are used. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (contract performance).
PrivacyPolicy.localStorageText:
Our website stores certain data in your browser's localStorage and sessionStorage for functional purposes. This includes:

— Your Klaro! consent preferences
— Anonymous player identifiers for multiplayer games
— Guest project data (unsaved chess boards, pieces, and rules created without an account)
— Board editor state and game engine settings
— AI chat history per project (sessionStorage, cleared when you close the browser)
— Anonymous visitor analytics (visitor ID in localStorage, session ID in sessionStorage) — used only to count unique visitors and sessions

These storage mechanisms are technically necessary for the features you actively use. No personal data is stored in localStorage or sessionStorage. All identifiers used for analytics are anonymous and cannot be linked to you as an individual. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in understanding aggregate visitor numbers to improve our service).
PrivacyPolicy.emailText: When you create an account with your email address, we store your display name, email address, and your password (hashed by Firebase). Optionally, you may provide a profile picture URL. This data is not public and is used exclusively to provide your account and platform features. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (contract performance).
PrivacyPolicy.googleText: You may also sign in using your Google account. In this case, Google transmits your name, email address, and profile picture to us. Google's own privacy policy applies to the authentication process. We do not receive your Google password. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR (consent) and Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (contract performance).
PrivacyPolicy.verificationText: Firebase sends automated emails for email verification (upon signup) and password reset (upon request). These emails are sent directly by Google's Firebase infrastructure. Your email address is transmitted to Firebase for this purpose only. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
PrivacyPolicy.firestoreText:
We use Google Firebase Cloud Firestore as our primary database. The following categories of your data are stored there:

— Account data (display name, email, profile picture, auth provider, creation date)
— User-created content (chess boards, piece sets, game logic)
— Game history and player statistics (games played, wins, losses, draws, rating)
— Marketplace items you publish (title, description, previews, ratings, reviews)
— Creator profiles (handle, display name, bio, photo)
— Community feedback and feature requests
— Anonymous usage counters (feature button clicks)

No payment data is stored or processed by us. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
PrivacyPolicy.firebaseStorageText: When you upload custom piece images in the creator tools, these files are stored in Google Firebase Cloud Storage. Only images you explicitly upload are stored. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
PrivacyPolicy.dpfText: Google LLC is certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework (DPF), ensuring an adequate level of data protection for transfers to the USA. Additionally, standard contractual clauses and supplementary technical and organizational measures are in place. You may request a copy of these safeguards by contacting us.
PrivacyPolicy.aiText:
We offer an AI-powered assistant that can help you design chess variants. When you interact with the AI, your chat messages, your current project state (board layout, pieces, and game rules), and system instructions are sent to a third-party AI provider for processing. Depending on configuration, this may be:

— DeepSeek (based in China, API endpoint: api.deepseek.com)
— OpenRouter (OpenRouter Inc., based in the USA, API endpoint: openrouter.ai)

The AI provider processes the transmitted data solely to generate responses and does not use it to train models. Chat history is temporarily stored in your browser's sessionStorage (per project, cleared when you close the browser) and may be persisted server-side for job recovery. We do not send personal identifiers to the AI provider — only your chat messages and anonymized project data from the current conversation are transmitted. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR (consent, implied by your use of the AI feature).
PrivacyPolicy.socketText: Our real-time multiplayer feature uses Socket.io, an open-source WebSocket library. When you join or create a multiplayer game, an anonymous player identifier is generated and stored in your browser's localStorage. This ID is not linked to your account and is only used to maintain your connection during the game session. Connection data (IP address) is processed transiently to establish and maintain the WebSocket connection. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
PrivacyPolicy.stockfishText: During multiplayer games, a server-side instance of the Stockfish chess engine may analyze board positions upon your request. Best-move suggestions are transmitted via the Socket.io connection to your browser. No personal data is sent to the engine — only the current board position. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
PrivacyPolicy.resendText:
We use Resend (Resend Inc., USA) to send transactional emails. These are limited to:

— Marketplace item report notifications (sent to our admin email)
— Referral survey responses (sent to our admin email)

Your email address is not transmitted to Resend for end-user communication. Admin notifications contain only the content of your report or survey response. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in platform safety and improvement).
PrivacyPolicy.firebaseEmailText: Automated emails (email verification and password reset) are sent via Google's Firebase infrastructure. See Section 5c above. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
PrivacyPolicy.ugcText:
Chessperiment includes public-facing features where content you create may be visible to other users:

— Marketplace: Boards, piece sets, and designs you publish are publicly visible along with their title, description, and preview image. Other users can rate, review, and fork your published items.
— Creator Profiles: If you register as a creator, your public profile (handle, display name, bio, photo) is visible to other users.
— Reviews & Ratings: Reviews and ratings you leave are publicly visible along with your display name.

You control what you publish. Unpublished content remains private. Published content that has been forked by other users may persist after you delete your account, as it becomes part of the forked project's history. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR (consent, given by publishing) and Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
PrivacyPolicy.communityText: Our feedback form on the Features page allows you to submit bug reports, feature requests, and general feedback. Submissions are stored in Firestore. You may optionally provide your email address for follow-up. Your IP address is processed in-memory only for rate limiting (maximum 5 submissions per hour) and is not stored. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in improving our service).
PrivacyPolicy.surveyText: A voluntary survey asks how you discovered Chessperiment. Your response is sent to our admin email via Resend (see Section 9a). No personal data is transmitted with your answer. Your dismissal of the survey is stored in localStorage to avoid showing it again. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR (consent, given by choosing to respond).
PrivacyPolicy.botidText: We use BotID, a bot detection service, on authentication-related endpoints to protect against automated abuse. BotID evaluates request patterns to distinguish between human users and bots. No personal data is transmitted to BotID — the service operates based on request metadata. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in securing our service against automated attacks).
PrivacyPolicy.bmcText: Our website includes a link to our Buy Me a Coffee page (buymeacoffee.com/chessperiment), an external donation platform. Clicking this link takes you to Buy Me a Coffee's website, where their own privacy policy applies. We do not transmit any personal data to Buy Me a Coffee through this link.
PrivacyPolicy.googleImagesText: If you sign in with Google, your profile picture is loaded from Google's servers (lh3.googleusercontent.com). This image request is made directly by your browser. Google's privacy policy applies to this data transfer.
PrivacyPolicy.retentionText:
We store your personal data only as long as necessary for the purposes for which it was collected:

— Account data: Stored until you delete your account. You may request deletion at any time by contacting us.
— User-created content: Retained while your account exists. On account deletion, your personal content is removed. Content forked by other users may persist in their projects.
— Game history & statistics: Retained for the lifetime of your account to maintain your player profile.
— Marketplace items: On account deletion, published items are removed from the marketplace.
— Chat messages: Retained while your account exists.
— Community feedback: Retained as long as relevant for product improvement.

To request deletion of your account and associated data, email us at contact.chessperiment@gmail.com.
PrivacyPolicy.rightsText:
Under the GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

— Right of access (Art. 15 GDPR): You may request confirmation of whether we process your data and obtain a copy.
— Right to rectification (Art. 16 GDPR): You may request correction of inaccurate personal data.
— Right to erasure (Art. 17 GDPR): You may request deletion of your data, subject to legal retention obligations.
— Right to restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR): You may request restricted processing under certain conditions.
— Right to data portability (Art. 20 GDPR): You may receive your data in a structured, commonly used format.
— Right to withdraw consent (Art. 7(3) GDPR): You may withdraw consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of prior processing.

To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at contact.chessperiment@gmail.com. We will respond within the statutory period of one month.
PrivacyPolicy.objectionText:
You have the right to object, on grounds relating to your particular situation, at any time to the processing of your personal data which is based on Art. 6(1)(e) or (f) GDPR, including profiling based on those provisions. If you object, we will no longer process your personal data unless we can demonstrate compelling legitimate grounds for the processing which override your interests, rights, and freedoms, or the processing serves the establishment, exercise, or defense of legal claims.

To exercise your right to object, simply send an email to contact.chessperiment@gmail.com. No special form is required.
PrivacyPolicy.complaintText:
Without prejudice to any other administrative or judicial remedy, you have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority, in particular in the member state of your habitual residence, place of work, or place of the alleged infringement, if you believe that the processing of your personal data infringes the GDPR.

The competent supervisory authority for Chessperiment is:

Der Landesbeauftragte für den Datenschutz Niedersachsen
Prinzenstraße 5
30159 Hannover
Germany
Phone: +49 (0)511 120-4500
Email: poststelle@lfd.niedersachsen.de
Website: https://www.lfd.niedersachsen.de
PrivacyPolicy.minorsText: Our services are not directed at persons under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from minors without verifiable parental consent. If you are a parent or guardian and believe your child has provided us with personal data without your consent, please contact us and we will delete it.
PrivacyPolicy.changesText: We may update this privacy policy from time to time to reflect changes in our data processing practices or legal requirements. The current version is always available on this page. We encourage you to review it periodically.

About.title: About Chessperiment — Free Chess Variant Creator & Abstract Strategy Game Engine
About.description: Chessperiment is not a template library — it's real customization. A free, browser-based game design engine for creating custom chess variants and abstract strategy games. Compare Chessperiment with Chess Remix, ChessCraft, Tabletop Simulator, and PyChess.

Features.title: Feature Timeline — Chessperiment
Features.description: See what's being built at Chessperiment — one feature a day. Report bugs, request features, or share feedback.

Marketplace.title: Chessperiment Marketplace | Download Custom Chess Sets
Marketplace.description: Browse and download community-created chess boards and piece sets. Enhance your game with unique designs.

seoContent.heading: Discover and share custom chess assets
seoContent.text: The Chessperiment Marketplace is your central hub for community-created chess variants and abstract strategy games. Browse and download custom piece sets featuring fairy chess pieces, leapers, and riders with unique movement logic. Discover hexagonal chess boards, custom-shaped playing fields, and complete variant rule sets — every creation built with real customization, not just templates. All community creations are free to download and play.

Footer.title: Frequently Asked Questions
Footer.whatIsChessperimentAnswer: Chessperiment is a free, browser-based game design engine for creating custom chess variants and abstract strategy games — now with an AI variant generator. Describe your game idea in plain language, and the AI creates the full variant instantly for you. You bring the creativity; the AI handles the implementation.
Footer.canAIBuildVariant: Can AI build a chess variant for me?
Footer.canAIBuildVariantAnswer: Yes. Chessperiment has a built-in AI assistant that generates complete chess variants from simple descriptions. Just describe your idea — for example, 'chess where pawns explode when captured' — and the AI instantly configures the boards, pieces, and rules for you. You can then adjust the result in the visual editor however you like.
Footer.isItFreeAnswer: Yes. Chessperiment is completely free — no paywalls, no premium tiers, no ads. All features — including the piece editor, board editor, multiplayer, and marketplace — are free to use.
Footer.canIPlayFriendsAnswer: Yes. Create a room and share the code or link. Your friend joins in the browser — no account or installation needed.
Footer.whatIsLeaper: What is a leaper in chess?
Footer.whatIsLeaperAnswer: A leaper is a chess piece that moves to a fixed offset from its current square, regardless of what pieces are in between. The standard knight is a leaper. Chessperiment's piece editor lets you define any leaper pattern using a visual grid.
Footer.whatIsRider: What is a rider in chess?
Footer.whatIsRiderAnswer: A rider is a chess piece that slides along a vector until blocked or reaching the board edge. Rooks, bishops, and queens are all riders. Chessperiment lets you define custom rider directions.
Footer.whatIsFairyPiece: What is a fairy chess piece?
Footer.whatIsFairyPieceAnswer: A fairy chess piece is any non-standard piece used in chess variants. Common examples include the amazon (queen + knight), the camel (3,1-leaper), and the nightrider (rider along knight directions). Chessperiment's piece editor supports the full spectrum of fairy piece definitions.
Footer.canCreateHexBoards: Can I create hexagonal chess boards?
Footer.canCreateHexBoardsAnswer: Yes. Chessperiment's board editor supports full hexagonal board generation, letting you design hex chess variants and other hexagonal abstract strategy games.
Footer.diffFromChessRemix: How is Chessperiment different from Chess Remix?
Footer.diffFromChessRemixAnswer: Chess Remix locks many creation features behind a paywall and offers preset customization options. Chessperiment provides deeper functionality — including leaper/rider definitions, conditional triggers, hexagonal boards, and square-specific rule overrides — completely free. No templates — real customization.
Footer.diffFromChessCraft: How is Chessperiment different from ChessCraft?
Footer.diffFromChessCraftAnswer: ChessCraft is a mobile app with predefined piece templates. Chessperiment runs in any browser without installation and offers a deeper logic system: a Scratch-style visual block editor with state-based conditional triggers and complex multi-move sequences. Full customization, no templates.
Footer.hasStockfishAnswer: Yes. Chessperiment integrates the Stockfish chess engine and applies it to custom game states — including non-standard board topologies, custom piece definitions, and variant win conditions.
Footer.canDesignNonChess: Can I design abstract strategy games that aren't chess?
Footer.canDesignNonChessAnswer: Yes. Although Chessperiment uses chess as a baseline, the board editor and piece logic system support the full space of combinatorial, perfect-information abstract strategy games — including connection games, territorial games, and entirely novel rule systems.
Footer.whatIsMarketplaceAnswer: The Marketplace is a community library where users publish and share their custom chess variants, piece sets, and board designs. All content is free to download and play.
Footer.builtBy: Built with great attention to detail for chess enthusiasts.

Home.title: Chessperiment — AI-Powered Chess Variant Creator & Abstract Strategy Game Engine
Home.description: Describe your game idea — the AI creates the variant instantly for you. You bring the creativity, AI handles the implementation. No templates, real customization. Define pieces with visual logic blocks, build hexagonal boards, create leapers, riders, and fairy chess pieces. Free, browser-based, no coding required.
Home.h1: Chessperiment — AI-Powered Chess Variant Creator and Abstract Strategy Game Engine
Home.p: Chessperiment is a free, browser-based sandbox for designing custom chess variants and abstract strategy games. Simply describe your game idea — our AI creates the full variant instantly for you. You have the creative vision, the AI skips the boring setup step. The result is a complete Chessperiment project you can adjust however you like in the editor. Create pieces with a Scratch-style visual logic editor — define leaper and rider movement patterns, add conditional triggers, and program complex fairy chess piece behaviors without any coding knowledge. Design hexagonal or custom-shaped boards and test instantly with friends via room codes. Stockfish analysis is built in and works with your own rules.

Game.title: Play Chess Variants Online - Multiplayer Chess Games
Game.description: Play innovative chess variants online with friends. Multiplayer chess with custom boards and pieces. Free to play, no download required.

seoContent.heading: Experience the Next Generation of Chess Variants
seoContent.text: Join the Chessperiment community and play unique chess variants online with friends or against our built-in Stockfish AI. Our platform lets you use custom boards and piece sets designed by creators from around the world. Whether you want to test new piece logic you just created, or play a match with standard rules on an irregular board — our smooth multiplayer experience has you covered. Start a private match by sharing a room code, or join the lobby to find other players. With real-time move validation and advanced engine analysis, Chessperiment provides a robust environment for both casual play and competitive testing of new game mechanics.

Editor.title: Project Editor | Custom Chessperiment
Editor.description: Manage your custom chess projects. Create unique battlefields, design piece sets, and organize your creations.

PieceEditor.title: Visual Chess Piece Editor — Define Leaper, Rider & Fairy Chess Piece Logic
PieceEditor.description: Define custom chess piece movements with visual logic blocks — no coding required. No templates — real customization. Create leapers, riders, and complex fairy chess pieces with conditional triggers, state-based capture rules, and multi-step move sequences.

SquareEditor.title: Square Rule Editor — Square-Specific Logic for Chess Variants
SquareEditor.description: Assign unique rules to individual board squares — create teleport squares, territory zones, promotion areas, or movement restrictions. Enables advanced chess variants including Fractal Chess.

BoardEditor.title: Chess Board Editor - Design Custom Boards
BoardEditor.description: Create your own chess board layouts. Customize dimensions, colors, and starting positions. Export or play directly.

seoContent.heading: Create and share custom chess boards
seoContent.text: With the Chessperiment board editor, design chess boards of any dimension — from compact 4x4 to expansive 14x14 grids or any custom size. Create full hexagonal boards for hex chess variants. Toggle individual squares to build non-rectangular shapes: rings, crosses, maps with internal obstacles, or any topology. Assign square-specific rule overrides to create territory zones, teleport squares, or restricted movement areas. These are not templates — this is real customization. Whether you're building a classic fairy chess variant, prototyping a Hex- or Y-style connection game, or designing an entirely new abstract strategy game from scratch — the board editor gives you full geometric control with no coding required.

Announcements.title: Chessperiment News & Announcements
Announcements.description: Stay up to date on the latest features, updates, and community news from Chessperiment.

Analyze.title: Analyze Chess Games with Stockfish
Analyze.description: Analyze your chess games with the powerful Stockfish engine. Improve your strategy and learn from your mistakes.

LegalNotice.title: Legal Notice - Chessperiment
LegalNotice.description: Legal notice (Impressum) for Chessperiment with all mandatory disclosures under § 5 DDG: contact details, legal representative, and consumer dispute resolution information.

Login.title: Sign in to Chessperiment | Save Your Creations
Login.description: Sign in to Chessperiment to save your custom chess boards and pieces and access your player profile.

PrivacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy – Chessperiment
PrivacyPolicy.description: How Chessperiment processes your personal data. GDPR-compliant transparency on accounts, AI services, cookies, Firebase, Klaro! consent management, and your data subject rights.

ProjectView.title: Project Overview | Custom Chessperiment
ProjectView.description: View and manage your chess project. Edit your board and pieces in one place.

PieceEditorFAQ.title: Piece Editor FAQ | Visual Scripting Help
PieceEditorFAQ.description: Learn how to use the piece editor, visual logic blocks, and movement rules. Detailed technical guide to creating custom chess pieces.

general.title: General
general.a1: The piece editor lets you create custom chess pieces with unique movement patterns and logic.
general.q2: How do I save my piece?
general.a2: Click the 'Save piece' button in the sidebar. Your piece will be saved in the current project.

moveEditor.title: Movement Editor
moveEditor.a1: Use the visual grid to click squares that the piece can move to relative to its position.
moveEditor.a2: Jumping moves allow the piece to leap over other pieces, like a knight.

logicEditor.title: Logic Editor
logicEditor.a1: It's a visual scripting environment where you can define complex behaviors using blocks.
logicEditor.a2: Triggers start a logic chain when a specific event occurs, such as 'onMove' or 'onCapture'.
logicEditor.a3: Yes, you can define and modify variables to create stateful pieces.
logicEditor.a4: You can move pieces, capture, create new ones, or modify game state variables.
logicEditor.a5: The 'explode' effect allows a piece to destroy all pieces within a certain radius. You can set the radius individually.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.a1: Check that you have defined valid moves in the movement or logic editor.
troubleshooting.a2: Make sure your blocks are connected correctly and the trigger condition is met.

NotFound.title: 404 - Piece Not Found
NotFound.description: It seems this move is invalid. The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.

About.title: About Chessperiment
About.intro: Chessperiment (chessperiment.app) is a web platform dedicated to creating highly customizable chess variants. Unlike conventional chess websites, Chessperiment allows users to design their own unique games — with full control over boards, pieces, rules, and interactions.

customBoards.title: Custom Boards
customBoards.description: Create boards in any shape and size that match your vision.

pieceEditor.title: Advanced Piece Editor
pieceEditor.description: Design pieces with unique movements and abilities.

visualScripting.title: Visual Scripting
visualScripting.description: Use drag-and-drop for triggers, effects, and variables — similar to Scratch — to create complex game mechanics without programming.

variants.title: User-Created Variants
variants.description: Experiment and play your own chess variants or try out the creations of others.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: Chessperiment exists to push the boundaries of creativity in chess. We want to give everyone, from beginners to advanced players, the ability to invent, share, and play entirely new chess experiences.

developer.title: About the Developer
developer.description: Chessperiment is developed by a solo developer who is active on Reddit and GitHub. Launched just a few weeks ago, the platform has already attracted over 100 users exploring the endless possibilities of customizable chess.

Donation.title: Help cover hosting
Donation.description: Chessperiment costs about €{amount}/month to keep online. If it helped you build, test, or play something fun, you can chip in.

ProjectView.boardViewComingSoon: Create a board to see it here

ExportVariant.title: Export Variant
ExportVariant.description: Export this variant as an image, image with piece descriptions, or as a JSON file for later import.
ExportVariant.imageOnlyDesc: PNG image of the board with pieces
ExportVariant.imageWithInfoDesc: PNG image with board and custom piece details
ExportVariant.fileDesc: JSON file to import on another account

Features.title: Feature Timeline
Features.subtitle: One feature a day — follow along as Chessperiment grows.
Features.empty: No features posted yet. Check back soon.

feedback.title: Your Voice
feedback.subtitle: Report a bug, request a feature, or just say something.
feedback.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

creator.title: My Published Content
creator.subtitle: Manage and customize your marketplace content
creator.noItemsDescription: Publish your first game, board, or piece set from the editor
creator.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this published content?
